dag.dag_name: Ecommerce real-time personalization cache exhausts Redis memory

tasks.dependencies: Estimates peak resource usage from row-count metadata before compute
Resource-intensive in-memory computation, historically prone to exhaustion
Checks actual peak usage against budget mid-run/post-run
Branch: resource usage within safe budget
Branch: near resource ceiling, reroutes to chunked processing

operator_config.sql: SELECT 1 AS autorca_probe, 'RTL_FCT_RES' AS dag_id, 'load_target' AS task_name;

tasks.dependencies: Logs final resource profile for trend monitoring

incident.incident_name: Ecommerce real-time personalization cache exhausts Redis memory
incident.description: Resource Exhaustion incident on the Forecasting pipeline (RTL_FCT_RES): ecommerce real-time personalization cache exhausts Redis memory.
incident.symptoms: The personalization-cache refresh task periodically fills the shared Redis instance to its maxmemory limit, triggering eviction of unrelated session-cache keys used by the live checkout flow.
incident.root_cause: The personalization DAG was never given its own Redis instance/namespace with a bounded key-eviction policy, so as the customer-segment cache grew (a new micro-segmentation feature added 40x more cache keys), it competed for memory with checkout session data on the same Redis cluster.
incident.business_impact: Checkout sessions were occasionally evicted from cache mid-session during the memory-pressure window, causing a small number of customers to be logged out or lose cart state during checkout, a direct, measurable revenue-risk event.
incident.resolution: Move personalization cache to a dedicated Redis instance with its own maxmemory and eviction policy, isolated from checkout-critical session data.

expected_rca.expected_rca_summary: AI AutoRCA should identify: The personalization DAG was never given its own Redis instance/namespace with a bounded key-eviction policy, so as the customer-segment cache grew (a new micro-segmentation feature added 40x more cache keys), it competed for memory with checkout session data on the same Redis cluster.
expected_rca.evidence_required: Airflow task logs, Datadog infrastructure/application metrics, JIRA/Confluence RAG context for known-issue matching
expected_rca.airflow_logs: Airflow task itself succeeds (no failure), but the AutoRCA correlation surfaces the downstream checkout impact via cross-service Datadog tagging
expected_rca.datadog_metrics: Datadog redis.memory.used approaching maxmemory with evicted_keys metric spiking, tagged keys show mixed personalization/session prefixes
expected_rca.resolution: Move personalization cache to a dedicated Redis instance with its own maxmemory and eviction policy, isolated from checkout-critical session data.
expected_rca.preventive_action: Require any shared-infrastructure resource (Redis, shared DB, shared queue) usage to be declared in the metadata model with an isolation/blast-radius assessment before production deployment.
